things.name: Wawel Royal Castle & Cathedral
things.description: Magnificent Renaissance castle on a limestone hill — the coronation site of Polish kings and queens

things.name: Main Market Square (Rynek Glowny)
things.description: One of medieval Europe's largest town squares with the Cloth Hall, St Mary's Basilica and hourly trumpeter

things.name: Auschwitz-Birkenau Memorial
things.description: Essential and sobering UNESCO World Heritage memorial to the Holocaust — 70 km from Krakow

things.name: Kazimierz Jewish Quarter
things.description: Medieval Jewish quarter revived with synagogues, galleries, cafes and Schindler's factory nearby

things.name: Wieliczka Salt Mine
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage underground salt mine with carved chapels, lakes and chambers 135 metres deep

things.name: Schindler's Factory Museum
things.description: Award-winning museum of wartime Krakow in Oskar Schindler's actual enamelware factory

things.name: St Mary's Basilica & Trumpeter
things.description: Glorious Gothic church with an altarpiece by Veit Stoss — the trumpeter plays the Hejnal every hour

things.name: Krakow Cloth Hall (Sukiennice)
things.description: Renaissance trading hall in the market square now housing Polish 19th-century art and souvenir stalls

things.name: Dragon's Den (Smocza Jama)
things.description: Limestone cave beneath Wawel Castle where the legendary Krakow Dragon once lived — fire-breathing statue outside

things.name: Tatra Mountains Day Trip
things.description: Poland's highest peaks 120 km south — take the cable car to Kasprowy Wierch for alpine hiking
